category_title: Elementary students with ADHD (Combined Type) exhibiting disruptive and inattentive behaviors
minimal_context: Alex is sitting near the back of the classroom and is asked to begin an independent academic assignment.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Alex, please take out your math worksheet and start working on the problems.

STUDENT: (Fidgeting with a toy and cracking his knuckles) My desk is a mess, I can't find my worksheet.

TUTOR: Please look inside your desk to find your materials.

STUDENT: (Standing beside his desk) I know I need to pay attention. I always get in trouble for talking too much.

TUTOR: Just find your worksheet and begin the assignment.

STUDENT: (Staring out the window) I want to look at clouds! The answer to the first question is ten!

options.A: Moving Alex to the front of the class, allowing for more prompting during lessons.
options.B: Implementing a progressive disciplinary sequence: first a warning, then a 10-minute timeout, followed by classroom suspension.
options.C: Establishing a classroom norm where disagreement is acceptable and providing a platform for evidence-based exchanges.
options.D: Pre-planning specific responses to potential non-compliance during independent academic assignments.
